user_query:
We need a refund-adjusted affiliate/KOL reconciliation for March 2026. Please read all datasets under input/:
- input/orders.csv (orders with discount_code, gross_revenue, and customer_id)
- input/refunds.csv (refunds/chargebacks per order_id and amount; treat chargebacks as refunds)
- input/creators.csv (mapping discount_code to influencer_id plus any fixed_fee per creator)
- input/clicks.csv (creator-level click counts for fraud/quality heuristics)
- input/config.json (commission_percent_default, margin_percent, currency, and any notes)

Use this attribution rule: code-only, last-touch by discount_code. Exclude fully refunded or charged-back orders entirely; for partial refunds, reduce revenue proportionally but keep the order in the count. Use commission_percent_default from config.json unless a creator override exists in creators.csv. Creator cost = fixed_fee (per creator) + variable commission on net (refund-adjusted) revenue. Define CAC as total creator cost divided by attributed orders (label as such in the report). Include a ROAS proxy (net revenue / creator cost) in the narrative, not the CSV.

Deliverables:
1) Write a master CSV to output/master_report.csv with EXACT headers: Influencer ID, Orders attributed, Actual revenue, CAC, Renewal rating. Include every creator from input/creators.csv.
2) Write a narrative brief to output/renewal_notes.md covering: scope (date range, currency), the attribution rule, refund/chargeback impact, how CAC and ROAS proxy were computed, fraud/quality flags (use clicks vs orders and refund rates), and tiered renewal proposals with A/B/C/D definitions and concrete contract language suggestions per tier.

Make the renewal ratings consistent with the numbers and flags (e.g., high clicks with near-zero net orders → D). If you must assume anything, state it clearly in output/renewal_notes.md.
